description: Reported crimes against persons and crimes against property based on National Incident Based Reporting System (NIBRS). Data reflects crime statistics reported to the Nevada Department of Public Safety on a monthly basis. Data is preliminary and subject to change without notice based on case updates. The City of Henderson Police Department became NIBRS certified in 2020 before the January 1, 2021 deadline mandated by the FBI who compiles and publishes the Uniform Crime Report. description: City-wide inventory of dataset publishing requests. Datasets published through the plan are those not associated with potentially sensitive, non-confidential information that can be provided in use-appropriate formats. The publishing plan is updated quarterly or as changes become necessary.

name: Water Distribution System Integrity

columns.name: Yearly Rolling Integrity
columns.description: Number of failures per 100 miles of distribution system

description: This indicator quantifies the condition of the water distribution system, expressed by the total number of leaks and pipeline breaks per 100 miles of distribution piping.

name: Non-Capacity Sewer Overflow Rate

columns.name: Yearly Rolling Rate
columns.description: Number of overflows per 100 miles of collection system piping

description: This indicator quantifies the condition and maintenance of the collection system, expressed by the total number of overflows per 100 miles of collection piping.

name: Utilities Past Due Accounts Receivable
description: Demonstrates outstanding past due utility balances. Late fees and water service interruptions for delinquent balances without a Payment Arrangement in good standing. Tracking began during COVID-19 on Jan 2019.

name: Increase Trails within 1/8 Mile of each Resident.
description: Increase Trails within 1/8 Mile of each Resident.

name: Attainment of ISO-14001 Env Mgt Certification
description: The City's commitment to mitigating environmental risk is demonstrated by its efforts to increase project implementation of the certified environmental management system using the ISO-14001 standard by 15% per fiscal year and attain the standard by FY23. The City has attained ISO- 14001 certification. No further data updates will be made.

description: Total number of permits (by category) broken down by last two months, same month previous year, year-to-date, and previous year-to-date to provide comparison. Also includes the value of the permits.

name: 10 Minute Walk to City Park
description: Percentage of residents within a 10-minute walk of a park

name: Henderson Performance Criteria Index Score
description: This strategic plan KPI is derived from the assessment the City receives due to the evaluation process for the Southwest Alliance for Excellence (SWAE) Performance Excellence Program, which utilizes the nationally recognized Baldrige criteria to identify opportunities for improvement and to evaluate performance excellence systematically. This metric is updated commensurate with each SWAE application.

columns.name: COH

description: Job growth is often used as a measure of economic expansion and health. The city's job growth consistently exceeds local competitors. Future job growth in Henderson is predicted to be 42.1%, higher than the US average of 33.5%. Note: Most current US Census data is 2018.

name: Increase Bus Stops within 1/4 Mile of each Resident.
description: Increase Bus Stops within 1/4 Mile of each Resident.

name: DUS Services Affordability
description: This indicator assesses affordability on disposable income based on the 20th income percentile minus essential expenses, rather than at median household income (per American Water Works Association's Measuring Household Affordability for Water and Sewer Utilities method of recommendation).

description: ETC Institute administered a survey to resident of the City of Henderson. The purpose of the survey was to gather information on residents’ level of satisfaction with City services and to gather residents’ opinions regarding various other topics. Input received will be used to help City leaders know where they are doing well and where they can improve; to make certain they are making decisions that align to the needs of residents and prioritizing services that will positively impact the communit

name: Rev under Budget
description: Revenue vs Budget (General Fund) FI - Strategic KPI Measure

name: Unemployment Rates
description: Unemployment Rates for Henderson, Las Vegas, and the United States. Figures are updated and reviewed monthly for general operations.

name: Cultural Event Attendance
description: Resident attendance at City special events

name: Redevelopment Tax Increment Revenue
description: This is the incremental revenue received by the Agency from the Clark County Treasurer for all real property taxes collected from all properties owned in a specific redevelopment area. The Agency is solely reliant on tax increment revenue for operations and to reinvest in designated redevelopment areas to provide financial incentives to developers, residents, and visitors in an effort to increase property values and eliminate blight.

name: Increase Number of New Jobs by 500 Per Year In Target Industry Sector
Resident Satisfaction with Emergency Preparedness
description: This measure demonstrates community satisfaction with emergency preparedness. Our goal is to increase community satisfaction with emergency preparedness to 85% by FY23. Our progress is measured by the Citizen Assessment Survey administered every 4 years.

name: RDA-Incremental Assessed Value
description: This is the incremental assessed value of all parcels located within a specific redevelopment area as assessed by the Clark County Assessor. Increases in assessed property value can be attributed to the focused efforts, activities, and public/private investment within a designated redevelopment area. Higher assessed values are a positive indicator of improvements, neighborhood desirability, and a strong economy.

name: Henderson City Boundary
description: Henderson City Boundary

name: Fitness Nutrition Health Program Participants
description: Residents participating in fitness, nutrition and health programs

name: Reduction of Environmental Impacts (E-Sign Processes)

columns.name: COH Electronic Documents to Team Member Ratio
Target COH Electronic Documents to Team Member Ratio

description: Senior leaders supported a pilot project for electronic document routing and signatures which resulted in citywide implementation of DocuSign technology to route contracts, forms, and a wide variety of other documents. E-signing approaches in government settings is the exception rather than the rule. The city has consistently increased its usage of electronic signing tools, increasing organizational efficiency and reducing environmental impacts.

name: Residential Notices of Default

columns.name: Month
columns.description: Month of default notice

columns.name: Value
columns.description: Number of default notices

description: The number of residential default notices. Residential Notices of Default is updated monthly for general operations review.

name: Share of 3- and 4-year-olds Enrolled in Preschool in Henderson

columns.name: No of 3 or 4 year olds in preschool

description: Percent of 3- and 4-year-olds enrolled in preschool in Henderson as reported annually on the American Community Survey.

name: Number of Businesses Assisted by City Staff
City Council Ward Boundaries
provider: City of Henderson GIS Data Portal
description: City of Henderson City Council Ward Boundaries.

name: Public Art Installations
description: Public art features implemented in the City

name: Team Member Productivity and Organizational Efficiency:  Employees per 1,000 Residents
description: To provide top-notch customer service and optimized processes, the city measures team member productivity, workforce capability and capacity, and process effectiveness and efficiency through its ratio of employees per 1,000 residents. Consistency is key and, as evidenced by the city's high levels of customer satisfaction in all service areas, the city's employee to resident ratio is achieving the optimum balance between customer service and lean operational approaches.

name: Percent of Henderson Preschools Participating in State Quality Rating Improvement System
description: Percentage of licensed child care centers in Henderson participating in the Quality Rating and Improvement System (QRIS).

name: SNWA Partnership Turf to Turf Conversion
description: These are estimates; the incentive is calculated by Southern Nevada Water Authority (SNWA) after measurements are taken.

name: Municipal Equality Index
description: The MEI examines how inclusive municipal laws, policies, and services are of LGBTQ+ people who live and work there. Cities are rated based on non-discrimination laws, the municipality as an employer, municipal services, law enforcement and leadership on LGBTQ+ equality.

columns.name: All Accidents Per 1000
columns.description: All traffic accidents per 1000

name: Median Household Income
description: Median household income is an important risk measure used by lenders for underwriting loans and is a useful economic indicator of an area's standard of living. The city's median household income is higher than all local comparisons. This may be due to the city's high quality of life and efforts to diversify the economy (attracting businesses from new, high-paying industries such as technology and manufacturing). Data Source: Community Development/Andrew Powell

name: Corporate Citizenship through Electrical Emissions Reductions
description: The State of Nevada elected to continue the Paris Climate Agreement, which outlines that City facilities must reduce emissions by 25-28% by 2025 (from the baseline year of 2005). The City of Henderson has reduced electrical emissions by 43%, and senior leaders are focused on remaining at 45,000 tons used per year even though it continues to add additional building square footage. Senior leaders support community quality of life by helping ensure good environmental stewardship and not only meetin

name: Average Math Proficiency Rate of Henderson Schools by School Year
description: Average Math proficiency rate for public schools, including both CCSD and charter, within the City of Henderson as reported annually in September by the Nevada Department of Education through the Nevada Accountability Portal. NOTE: Data reported annually in September represents the same year's graduating class (ie- September 2025 data represents the graduating class of 2025).

name: Park and Rec Center Art Installations
description: Art pieces installed in parks and recreation centers throughout the city. Includes murals, statues, painted benches, etc.

name: Average ELA Proficiency Rate of Henderson Schools by School Year
description: Average ELA proficiency rate for public schools, including both CCSD and charter, within the City of Henderson as reported annually in September by the Nevada Department of Education through the Nevada Accountability Portal. NOTE: Data reported annually in September represents the same year's graduating class (ie- September 2025 data represents the graduating class of 2025).

name: Number of City Education Events_archived

columns.name: Event
columns.description: Name of Event

description: The number of education events hosted or sponsored by the City of Henderson.

name: Percent of Henderson Schools with Star Rating of 3 or Higher

columns.name: Percent of Henderson Schools with Star Rating of 3 or Higher

description: Percent of public schools within the City of Henderson, including both CCSD and charter, with Star Rating of 3 or higher as reported annually by the Nevada Department of Education through the Nevada Accountability Portal. NOTE: Data reported annually in September represents the school year completed in the same calendar year (ie- September 2025 data represents the 2024-2025 school year).

name: Percent of NSHE Students from Henderson High Schools Requiring Supports for Math

columns.name: Percent of NSHE Students from Henderson High Schools Requiring Supports for Math

description: Percent of NSHE students from Henderson High Schools either enrolled in either Co-Requisite Math Support or Old Model Math Support, as reported annually in November by the NSHE Institutional Research Office. NOTE: Data reported annually in November represents the prior year's graduating class (ie- November 2025 data represents the graduating class of 2024).

name: Percent of NSHE Students from Henderson High Schools Requiring Supports for English

columns.name: Percent of NSHE Students from Henderson High Schools Requiring Supports for English

description: Percent of NSHE students from Henderson High Schools either enrolled in either Co-Requisite English Support or Old Model English Support, as reported annually in November by the NSHE Institutional Research Office. NOTE: Data reported annually in November represents the prior year's graduating class (ie- November 2025 data represents the graduating class of 2024).
